items.title: Single-cell RNA clusters in 2D
items.scenario: A biologist has 20,000 cells × 2,000 gene counts. PCA scatter shows one blob; after StandardScaler, UMAP with n_neighbors=30 and min_dist=0.1 reveals distinct cell-type islands that match known markers.
items.explanation: High-dimensional gene expression lies on a curved manifold; UMAP's fuzzy graph captures cell-type neighborhoods PCA's linear projection smears together.

items.title: Word embeddings of product reviews
items.scenario: A data scientist embeds 5,000 review sentences with a sentence transformer (384 dims), runs UMAP to 2D, and colors points by star rating. n_neighbors=15 separates rating bands locally; raising n_neighbors to 100 connects topical branches across ratings.
items.explanation: n_neighbors tunes how much local rating clusters vs broader semantic branches appear in the plot.

items.title: min_dist separates overlapping clusters
items.scenario: On MNIST digits projected with UMAP, min_dist=0.0 yields tight digit islands with little overlap; min_dist=0.8 spreads digits so global relationships (similar stroke styles) remain visible but clusters bleed together.
items.explanation: min_dist is an embedding-space knob—lower values tighten clusters; higher values preserve more global spacing.

items.title: PCA baseline before UMAP on tabular fraud data
items.scenario: An analyst plots fraud vs normal transactions on PCA PC1–PC2—heavy overlap. UMAP on scaled features shows a thin fraudulent branch PCA missed, but axis units are no longer interpretable as variance loadings.
items.explanation: Compare with PCA for linear baseline; use UMAP when non-linear separation is the visualization goal, not when you need explained-variance diagnostics.
